sections.title: 📖 What is Projectile Motion?
sections.content: Projectile motion is the motion of an object thrown into the air, subject to only the acceleration of gravity. The object is called a **projectile**, and its path is called its **trajectory**.
keyPoints: No air resistance is assumed (ideal case)

sections.title: 📐 Key Equations
sections.content: For a projectile launched with initial velocity v₀ at angle θ:

formulas.name: Vertical Position
formulas.formula: y = v₀ · t · sin(θ) - ½ · g · t²

sections.title: 💡 Important Physics Concepts
points: Horizontal velocity (vₓ) remains **constant** throughout the flight
Vertical velocity (vᵧ) changes at rate **g** (acceleration due to gravity)
Flight time depends only on **vertical component** of velocity

sections.title: 🌍 Real-World Applications
examples: 🏏 Cricket: When a batsman hits a six

sections.title: 📝 Derivations (Class 11-12)
sections.content: Understanding where the formulas come from:
steps: From equations of motion: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at²
At maximum height: vᵧ = 0 → t = v₀·sin(θ)/g
Total flight time = 2 × time to max height
Range = vₓ × total time = v₀·cos(θ) × 2v₀·sin(θ)/g = v₀²·sin(2θ)/g
